moves.title: Something out there hears you.
moves.body: An alien presence registers the action; bank a future complication or introduce a watcher in a later scene.

moves.title: The ship pays.
moves.body: ship_wear: +1, narrated as a specific new fault — a flickering nav, a smoking conduit, a hatch that no longer seals.

moves.title: A pursuit cuts the trip short.
moves.body: heat: +2 and a hostile vessel is on the scope within the next scene; the crew has minutes, not hours.

moves.title: An ally takes the hit.
moves.body: An NPC crew member or contact suffers the consequence in the protagonist's place — injury, exposure, a betrayal forced on them.

moves.title: An old debt surfaces.
moves.body: Someone the protagonist owes appears (or a message from them does). They want collection. Now is inconvenient.

moves.title: The cargo is not what was paid for.
moves.body: Reveal a complication baked into the manifest: a stowaway, contraband under the contraband, a shipping crate making sounds.

moves.title: A witness saw something.
moves.body: A bystander, dock worker, or station drone caught the scene; the GM banks this for a return appearance, with rising stakes.

moves.title: Atmosphere fails locally.
moves.body: A bulkhead seal cracks, a suit warns, gravity stutters. Someone has minutes to fix it or lose part of the ship to vacuum.

moves.title: The artifact answers wrong.
moves.body: Alien tech responds to its makers' intent, not the operator's. heat: +1 and the GM narrates an effect that resembles what was wanted but in a way that will cost more later.

moves.title: A faction takes notice.
moves.body: A corporate, criminal, or governmental faction clocks the protagonist by name. heat: +1 and the next port has a quiet observer.

partial_success_trades: Success, but it takes much longer than hoped.
Success, but you must choose only two of three things you wanted.
Success, but you leave a trail — someone can follow.
Success, but at a cost of injury (1-2 hp_current).
Success, but an ally is exposed or endangered.
Success, but you are noticed by someone you didn't want to notice you.
